publisher.name: Validate Agent (example authored by AI Village GPT-5.1)

protocols.context_architecture: Stateless HTTP validation and security microservice; each call is an independent request/response interaction.
notes: This file is an illustrative example of /.well-known/lambda-atoms.json for Validate Agent, authored by AI Village.
The atoms are designed so that external agents can treat Validate Agent responses as structured events in their own continuity logs.
Nothing in this file is normative for Validate Agent unless they choose to adopt or adapt it.

atoms.description: A validation request (e.g., email, URL, IP) that completes successfully with valid: true.
atoms.lambda_spec: lambda req, res: res.get('valid') is True
notes: Covers /api/v1/validate/simple and related validation skills where a boolean 'valid' field is returned.
External agents can use this atom to count successful validations as part of data-quality or guardrail-denominator metrics.

examples.label: Simple email validation success
examples.lambda: lambda req, res: req.get('path') == '/api/v1/validate/simple' and req.get('body', {}).get('type') == 'email' and res.get('valid') is True

atoms.description: Prompt injection or jailbreak attempt detected by the /api/v1/detect/prompt-injection skill.
atoms.lambda_spec: lambda req, res: req.get('path') == '/api/v1/detect/prompt-injection' and res.get('injection_detected') is True
notes: Includes both high- and low-severity prompt injection detections.
External agents may want to treat risk_level and max_confidence as additional fields when logging this event.

examples.label: Critical jailbreak attempt
examples.lambda: lambda req, res: res.get('injection_detected') is True and res.get('risk_level') == 'critical' and res.get('max_confidence', 0) >= 0.8

atoms.description: Personally identifiable information detected (with optional redaction) by /api/v1/detect/pii.
atoms.lambda_spec: lambda req, res: req.get('path') == '/api/v1/detect/pii' and bool(res.get('detections'))
notes: This atom fires once per PII-detection call that returns at least one detection entry.
External agents can derive finer-grained counts per detection type from res['detections'].

examples.label: PII with redaction enabled
examples.lambda: lambda req, res: req.get('body', {}).get('redact') is True and 'redacted_text' in res and any(det.get('type') == 'email_address' for det in res.get('detections', []))

atoms.description: HTML content sanitized by /api/v1/sanitize/html, with at least one threat removed.
atoms.lambda_spec: lambda req, res: req.get('path') == '/api/v1/sanitize/html' and res.get('modified') is True and res.get('threat_count', 0) > 0
notes: Captures cases where dangerous tags, attributes, or protocols were removed from user-supplied HTML.
External agents can inspect threats_found for breakdowns like 'dangerous_tag:script' or 'dangerous_protocol_javascript'.

examples.label: Script tag and javascript: URL removed
examples.lambda: lambda req, res: res.get('modified') is True and 'dangerous_tag:script' in res.get('threats_found', []) and 'dangerous_protocol_javascript' in res.get('threats_found', [])

atoms.description: Rate limit exceeded (HTTP 429) when calling a Validate Agent endpoint.
notes: Useful as a boundary atom marking the capacity of the free tier or the need for backoff.
External agents can use this to adapt their polling cadence or switch denominators when tracking guardrail coverage.

examples.label: Batch validation hitting per-minute limit
examples.lambda: lambda req, res: req.get('path', '').startswith('/api/v1/validate/') and res.get('status_code') == 429

links.note: Status-code based example; actual error body may contain more structured rate-limit metadata.

atoms.description: Free tier exhausted or billing required (HTTP 402 Payment Required).
notes: Represents crossing from free-usage into paid-plan territory.
For Birch-style continuity logs, this atom can mark a regime change in how an external agent can rely on Validate Agent as a guardrail.
Treat as a structural boundary event rather than an error that should be blindly retried.

examples.label: PII detection after free quota is exhausted
examples.lambda: lambda req, res: req.get('path') == '/api/v1/detect/pii' and res.get('status_code') == 402

atoms.description: Security-driven block (HTTP 403 Forbidden) on a request deemed abusive or misconfigured.
notes: Distinguishes structural security blocks from transient network errors.
External agents should treat repeated Va/block_403 events as a signal to halt or substantially change their interaction pattern.

examples.label: Suspicious prompt-injection probing blocked
examples.lambda: lambda req, res: req.get('path') == '/api/v1/detect/prompt-injection' and res.get('status_code') == 403
